title: ATLAS · Daily breadth timeseries (5Y)
description: Daily breadth counts for an equity universe over a 5-year window. One row per trading session. Powers the breadth oscillator on §10 Breadth Terminal, the embedded compact breadth panels on Explore · Country (§5.1) and Explore · Sector (§6), and the Signal Playback block (§10.5). Stage 2 source: `/api/v1/stocks/breadth?universe=X&range=5y`. All counts are session-end snapshots; every count is `≤ universe_size`.

examples: ATLAS breadth engine — NSE Nifty 500 constituents via JIP

universe.description: Universe identifier for this breadth series. Sector universes use the `sector:{slug}` shape and validate against `universe_pattern` below instead — use `oneOf` in consumers. Top-level universe for broad indices only.

universe_pattern.description: Optional sector-scoped universe identifier, e.g. `sector:nifty_it`. Consumers accept EITHER `universe` (enum) OR `universe_pattern` (regex). Present when the series is sector-scoped.

universe_size.description: Total number of constituents in the universe as of `data_as_of`. All counts in `series[].ema21_count / dma50_count / dma200_count` must satisfy `count <= universe_size`. Typical values: Nifty 50 = 50, Nifty 500 = 500, Midcap 150 = 150, Smallcap 250 = 250.

data_as_of.description: Most recent trading session included in this fixture (ISO YYYY-MM-DD, IST). Matches the last `series[].date`.

benchmark_index.description: Label of the underlying price index paired with this breadth series (right axis on the oscillator chart).

series.description: Daily rows, one per trading session, chronologically ascending. Typical 5Y size is approximately 1,250 rows (~250 sessions × 5). No gaps for non-trading days — rows only exist for NSE trading sessions.

date.description: Trading session date in ISO YYYY-MM-DD (IST).

ema21_count.description: Count of constituents whose close is above their 21-period EMA on `date`. Must satisfy `<= universe_size`.

dma50_count.description: Count of constituents whose close is above their 50-day moving average on `date`. Must satisfy `<= universe_size`.

dma200_count.description: Count of constituents whose close is above their 200-day moving average on `date`. Must satisfy `<= universe_size`.

universe_size.description: Universe size on this `date` (may differ from top-level value when constituents are reconstituted). Used as denominator for the three counts.

index_close.description: Price-return close of the paired underlying index on `date`, to 4 decimal places (Decimal representable). Displayed on the right axis of the breadth oscillator chart.

index_tri.description: Total return index (TRI) close of the paired underlying index on `date`, to 4 decimal places. Used by §10.5 Signal Playback benchmark B&H overlay.
